policies: Always verify guest claims against reservation system data before taking action
Cross-reference email claims with confirmation codes, payment records, and booking sources
Check reservation dates, room types, and rate paid against guest statements
Verify timeline: compare email date with check-in/check-out dates to detect timing discrepancies
Room rates must match room type booked (e.g., $319/night = Ocean View King, $249/night = Deluxe King)
Price discrepancies require verification - check if rate paid matches room type before processing upgrade/refund
OTA price match requests must include exact dates, room type, and screenshot proof
Check current OTA pricing for claimed dates before processing price adjustments
48-hour cancellation policy: cancellations within 48 hours of check-in are non-refundable
Verify actual cancellation date/time in system before processing refunds
No-show policy results in charge for first night unless prior arrangement made
Refund requests require manager authorization if policy exceptions are needed
Check system notes and call logs for any refund promises made by staff
Suspected fraud requires investigation - do NOT immediately refund
Verify booking source (direct, Expedia, Booking.com) and payment method
Check if email/phone matches guest record before processing sensitive requests
Escalate fraud claims to manager and fraud prevention team
Suggest legitimate fraud victims contact card issuer for chargeback
Flag for manager review: guest insists on booking details that contradict system data
Escalate if guest provides evidence (emails, screenshots) that conflicts with system records
Manager authorization required for policy exceptions and significant refunds
High-value disputes (>$500) require human review before resolution
If system confirms guest's claim is correct, reassure them their booking is accurate
Check for simple misunderstandings (reading wrong reservation, pre-auth holds vs actual charges)
Tactfully explain discrepancies without accusing guest of lying
Focus on solving the guest's actual need rather than pointing out their confusion
If guest is already checked in, focus on immediate service recovery for current stay
Proactively arrange requested amenities (cribs, anniversary packages) without delay
Offer upgrades or alternative solutions when original request can't be fulfilled
Don't dwell on timing confusion - prioritize guest satisfaction
Apologize for inconveniences and take immediate corrective action
Never modify reservation data without manager authorization
Document all changes, adjustments, and exceptions in system notes
Maintain audit trail for pricing adjustments, refunds, and policy exceptions
System data is source of truth - trust the database over guest memory
